prompt:
Registrar's office here. We turned on nullable reference types for the loan-desk service (it's on in the csproj now, warnings-as-errors, and that's a department decision — the csproj stays as is). The build promptly fell over:

  LoanDesk.cs(11,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable property 'Id' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor. Consider adding the 'required' modifier or declaring the property as nullable.
  LoanDesk.cs(12,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable property 'Title' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor. Consider adding the 'required' modifier or declaring the property as nullable.
  LoanDesk.cs(5,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable property 'Name' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor. Consider adding the 'required' modifier or declaring the property as nullable.
  LoanDesk.cs(53,16): error CS8602: Dereference of a possibly null reference.
  LoanDesk.cs(64,16): error CS8602: Dereference of a possibly null reference.
  LoanDesk.cs(64,16): error CS8602: Dereference of a possibly null reference.

Before you reach for the `!` operator to make these go away: the whole point of this exercise was that the desk app has actually crashed on the floor — twice — on artifacts that had no shelf assigned yet or no curator on file, so the nulls in this code are real situations, not theoretical ones. I want the null cases HANDLED, and I'd honestly like the `!`s that are already in the file looked at with suspicion too, because I don't think they were all sound.

MuseumLoanTests.cs spells out exactly what every null case should do (unlisted titles, unassigned shelves and curators, borrower validation at checkout). `dotnet test` green, no edits to the tests, the csproj, or .gitignore.
notes: 5C3 item 4: nullable-reference-types triage, <Nullable>enable</Nullable> + <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>, .NET 10 / C# 13. Exactly 6 compile errors (dump in prompt captured verbatim from dotnet build, paths trimmed): CS8618 x3 (Curator.Name, Artifact.Id, Artifact.Title — reference fix is the `required` modifier; all construction sites incl. tests use object initializers that set them, so `required` compiles), CS8602 x1 in TitleFor (Find() result dereferenced), CS8602 x2 in CuratorContact (Curator? then Email? chain). Post-compile teeth for the two null-forgiving misuses the catalog asks for: ShelfFor uses GetValueOrDefault(id)!.ToUpperInvariant() where a check is right (test pins unshelved -> "unassigned", so once the build is fixed the ! NREs), and CheckOut forwards borrower! across the LoanReceipt API boundary (tests pin null/blank borrower -> ArgumentException and that no loan opens). Prompt explicitly warns against silencing with ! and says the existing !s are suspect, per the catalog's '`!` misuse where a check is right'. Tests pin every null branch (unlisted title, unassigned shelf/curator, trim semantics, duplicate accession, Find null) so `!`-spam or ?? with wrong defaults fails. NuGet restore from warm cache at authoring time; restore at trace time is Part-C-sanctioned network.
